# HelpChatbot — RAG-Powered Documentation Chatbot

## Problem

Build a Blazor web chatbot that answers customer questions using internal product PDF documentation. The system must ingest PDFs from a local folder, store embeddings in Postgres (pgvector), and use an LLM to generate grounded answers with source attribution.

## Approach

**RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)** pipeline built on the Microsoft.Extensions.AI abstraction layer with pgvector for vector search and Blazor Server for the UI.

### Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology | Rationale |
|-------|-----------|-----------|
| Web UI | Blazor Server (Interactive) | Real-time streaming chat; C# end-to-end |
| LLM Abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic; simple prompt→response (no agentic loops needed) |
| Embeddings | Microsoft.Extensions.AI (`IEmbeddingGenerator`) | Same abstraction layer for embeddings |
| LLM Provider | OpenAI (swappable via DI) | Default provider; can swap to Azure OpenAI or Ollama |
| Vector Store | Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData + Npgsql pgvector provider | Postgres-native vector search; matches the Postgres requirement |
| PDF Parsing | UglyToad.PdfPig | Open-source .NET PDF text extraction |
| Database / ORM | EF Core + Npgsql | Chat history, ingestion tracking in Postgres |
| Hosting | ASP.NET Core (.NET 10) | Already scaffolded |

### Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐      ┌──────────────────┐      ┌─────────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │─────▶│  ChatService     │─────▶│  IChatClient│──▶ LLM
│  (Chat Page) │      │  (RAG pipeline)  │      └─────────────┘
└──────────────┘      │                  │
                      │  1. Embed query  │──▶ IEmbeddingGenerator
                      │  2. Vector search│──▶ pgvector (Postgres)
                      │  3. Build prompt │
                      │  4. Stream answer│
                      └──────────────────┘

┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Ingestion Pipeline (startup/on-demand)  │
│  PDF → PdfPig → Chunk → Embed → pgvector│
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Add NuGet packages: Microsoft.Extensions.AI, OpenAI, Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions, Npgsql vector data provider, UglyToad.PdfPig, EF Core + Npgsql, Blazor component libraries
- Configure `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string and AI settings (API key via user-secrets)

### 2. Data model & EF Core setup
- `DocumentChunk` entity: Id, DocumentName, ChunkIndex, Text, Embedding (vector)
- `ChatMessage` entity: Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp
- `AppDbContext` with pgvector extension enabled
- EF Core migration for initial schema

### 3. PDF ingestion service
- `PdfIngestionService`: reads PDFs from `Docs/` folder
- Text extraction via PdfPig
- Semantic chunking (split on paragraphs/sections, ~500 token chunks with overlap)
- Batch embedding generation via `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- Upsert chunks into pgvector via MEVD abstractions
- Track which files have been ingested to avoid re-processing

### 4. RAG query service
- `ChatService`: accepts user question, returns streamed answer
- Embed the user query
- Vector similarity search (top 5 chunks, minimum score threshold 0.75)
- Build system prompt with retrieved context + source attribution instructions
- Stream LLM response back via `IChatClient`
- Persist chat messages to Postgres

### 5. Blazor chat UI
- `Chat.razor` page: chat interface with message history
- Streaming response display (token-by-token)
- Source/reference display showing which document chunks were used
- Session management (new chat, clear history)
- Basic responsive styling

### 6. DI registration & configuration
- Register `IChatClient` (OpenAI provider) with retry logic
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embeddings
- Register vector store with pgvector
- Register EF Core context
- Register application services
- Configure options pattern for AI settings

### 7. Startup & ingestion trigger
- Run EF Core migrations on startup (dev mode)
- Trigger PDF ingestion on first launch or via admin endpoint
- Health check for Postgres and OpenAI connectivity

## Notes & Guardrails

- **Temperature 0** for factual documentation Q&A (deterministic answers)
- **Pin model versions** (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18` for cost efficiency)
- **No agentic framework needed** — this is single prompt→response with RAG context; `IChatClient` is the correct layer
- **Relevance threshold** on vector search to avoid injecting irrelevant chunks
- **Source attribution** — every answer must cite which document/section it drew from
- **Secrets management** — API keys via user-secrets (dev) / env vars (prod), never in committed config
- **Streaming** — use `GetStreamingResponseAsync` for time-to-first-token UX
- **Embedding caching** — chunks are embedded once at ingestion time, not re-embedded per query

### Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology | Rationale |
|-------|-----------|-----------|
| Web UI | Blazor Server (Interactive) | Real-time streaming chat; C# end-to-end |
| LLM Abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic; simple prompt→response (no agentic loops needed) |
| Embeddings | Microsoft.Extensions.AI (`IEmbeddingGenerator`) | Same abstraction layer for embeddings |
| LLM Provider | OpenAI (swappable via DI) | Default provider; can swap to Azure OpenAI or Ollama |
| Vector Store | Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData + Npgsql pgvector provider | Postgres-native vector search; matches the Postgres requirement |
| PDF Parsing | UglyToad.PdfPig | Open-source .NET PDF text extraction |
| Database / ORM | EF Core + Npgsql | Chat history, ingestion tracking in Postgres |
| Hosting | ASP.NET Core (.NET 10) | Already scaffolded |

### Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐      ┌──────────────────┐      ┌─────────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │─────▶│  ChatService     │─────▶│  IChatClient│──▶ LLM
│  (Chat Page) │      │  (RAG pipeline)  │      └─────────────┘
└──────────────┘      │                  │
                      │  1. Embed query  │──▶ IEmbeddingGenerator
                      │  2. Vector search│──▶ pgvector (Postgres)
                      │  3. Build prompt │
                      │  4. Stream answer│
                      └──────────────────┘

┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Ingestion Pipeline (startup/on-demand)  │
│  PDF → PdfPig → Chunk → Embed → pgvector│
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Add NuGet packages: Microsoft.Extensions.AI, OpenAI, Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions, Npgsql vector data provider, UglyToad.PdfPig, EF Core + Npgsql, Blazor component libraries
- Configure `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string and AI settings (API key via user-secrets)

### 2. Data model & EF Core setup
- `DocumentChunk` entity: Id, DocumentName, ChunkIndex, Text, Embedding (vector)
- `ChatMessage` entity: Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp
- `AppDbContext` with pgvector extension enabled
- EF Core migration for initial schema

### 3. PDF ingestion service
- `PdfIngestionService`: reads PDFs from `Docs/` folder
- Text extraction via PdfPig
- Semantic chunking (split on paragraphs/sections, ~500 token chunks with overlap)
- Batch embedding generation via `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- Upsert chunks into pgvector via MEVD abstractions
- Track which files have been ingested to avoid re-processing

### 4. RAG query service
- `ChatService`: accepts user question, returns streamed answer
- Embed the user query
- Vector similarity search (top 5 chunks, minimum score threshold 0.75)
- Build system prompt with retrieved context + source attribution instructions
- Stream LLM response back via `IChatClient`
- Persist chat messages to Postgres

### 5. Blazor chat UI
- `Chat.razor` page: chat interface with message history
- Streaming response display (token-by-token)
- Source/reference display showing which document chunks were used
- Session management (new chat, clear history)
- Basic responsive styling

### 6. DI registration & configuration
- Register `IChatClient` (OpenAI provider) with retry logic
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embeddings
- Register vector store with pgvector
- Register EF Core context
- Register application services
- Configure options pattern for AI settings

### 7. Startup & ingestion trigger
- Run EF Core migrations on startup (dev mode)
- Trigger PDF ingestion on first launch or via admin endpoint
- Health check for Postgres and OpenAI connectivity

## Notes & Guardrails

- **Temperature 0** for factual documentation Q&A (deterministic answers)
- **Pin model versions** (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18` for cost efficiency)
- **No agentic framework needed** — this is single prompt→response with RAG context; `IChatClient` is the correct layer
- **Relevance threshold** on vector search to avoid injecting irrelevant chunks
- **Source attribution** — every answer must cite which document/section it drew from
- **Secrets management** — API keys via user-secrets (dev) / env vars (prod), never in committed config
- **Streaming** — use `GetStreamingResponseAsync` for time-to-first-token UX
- **Embedding caching** — chunks are embedded once at ingestion time, not re-embedded per query
